_metadata.match: Orlando City SC vs Houston Dynamo FC

el_paso_recap.red_card: Kofi Twumasi (El Paso) 25' — shoved Lingr running into the box. El Paso played 65+ minutes with 10 men.
el_paso_recap.next_open_cup: Round of 16 — Louisville City FC at Shell Energy Stadium, April 28 or 29

injury_report.artur: OUT — preseason knee injury, no return date
injury_report.mcglynn: QUESTIONABLE — game-time decision, missed last 2 MLS matches (lower body)
injury_report.markanich: AVAILABLE — suspension fully served, scored vs El Paso on Wednesday

predicted_xi_bcs.source: Dustyn Richardson, Bayou City Soccer / The Mane Land cross-interview

griffin_dorsey_revenge.detail: Houston traded Griffin Dorsey to Orlando on Feb 18 for $1M in allocation money. Dynamo statement: 'We wish Griffin and his wife, Adriana, all the very best.' Now he could play against his old team tonight.

both_teams_in_crisis.orlando: 1W 1D 5L from 7 matches. Lost 5-0 to Nashville, 6-0 to LAFC. Conceded in 23 straight regular season matches — longest active streak in MLS, longest in club history, tied 14th longest ever.
both_teams_in_crisis.houston: 2-4-0, 12th in West. Lost by 4+ in last away match (6-2 at Colorado). Conceded 10 goals in first 2 away matches — tied for most in MLS history.

guilherme_historic_pace.detail: 9 goal contributions (5G/4A) in 6 matches — tied with Rafael Navarro for most in MLS. First Dynamo player to reach 9 contributions in first 6 matches since Brian Ching in 2006. That's a 20-year record.

opponent_scouting.form: Lost 5-0 to Nashville, 6-0 to LAFC. Drew 1-1 at Columbus last time out. Conceded in 23 straight regular season matches.
opponent_scouting.key_departure_from_houston: Griffin Dorsey — traded from Dynamo to Orlando Feb 18 for $1M GAM. Wife Adriana.

beat_writer_sources.author: Rudy Segura
beat_writer_sources.title: Three Players to Watch: Orlando City SC

beat_writer_sources.outlet: Bayou City Soccer / The Mane Land
beat_writer_sources.author: Dustyn Richardson / Ben Miller
beat_writer_sources.title: Previewing Houston Dynamo at Orlando City with The Mane Land

players_to_watch.name: Marco Pašalić
players_to_watch.why_interesting: One of Orlando's top scorers. Direct finisher with striker's instinct. Always looking to get forward. BCS says Houston defenders need to stay tight from the opening whistle.

players_to_watch.name: Martín Ojeda
players_to_watch.why_interesting: Orlando's most dangerous attacking player and Designated Player. Creates, scores, dictates tempo. 'If he gets time on the ball, he can completely change the rhythm of the game.' 8 assists and 4 goals — leads team in assists.

players_to_watch.name: Iván Angulo
players_to_watch.why_interesting: Pace and constant movement. 'Doesn't stop running.' 2 assists this season (most on the team). Colombian international. 46.6K followers. BCS says Houston needs to be sharp tracking his runs.

season_context.dynamo_record: 2-4-0 (6 pts from 6 MLS matches, 12th in West)
season_context.dynamo_open_cup: Beat El Paso 4-1 (Apr 15). Next: Louisville City, Apr 28-29.
season_context.key_stat_bcs: Houston has conceded first in EVERY MLS match this season. Both teams have conceded 10+ goals in their first 2 away matches — tied for worst in MLS history.
season_context.guilherme_stat: 5G/4A in 6 matches. 9 goal contributions = most in MLS (tied w/ Navarro). First Dynamo player to 9 in first 6 matches since Brian Ching in 2006.
season_context.defensive_note: Per Dustyn Richardson (BCS): 'They've been poor defensively, flat out. The offense can score with anyone but they can't keep teams from scoring, and in bunches. Olsen has shifted to a pronounced back three this season with growing pains. If Houston can get Andrade, Sviatchenko, and Carlos together consistently, this should help.'
